notes: 1959 brick block in Wola, post-renovation, ground floor, spółdzielcze własnościowe ownership form, czynsz 930 PLN (12.7 PLN/m² — within the normal 8–15 PLN/m² band, so we DON'T require it in §6 risks; the agent rightly treats it as an expense in §5, not a structural risk). Asking 1.29M PLN at 17,671 PLN/m². The reference case we've iterated on throughout development. Yield band 5.0–7.0 covers the full typical Warsaw range — earlier 6.5 ceiling broke on normal rent-comp drift (live Otodom data shifts the chosen rent benchmark by a few PLN/m² between runs).

Central-premium tier: a 42 m² 2-room flat in a 1930 pre-war kamienica on ul. Żurawia, Śródmieście, marketed for investment. Asking 795,000 PLN (18,929 PLN/m²), priced ~12% below the comp median — an unusual discount the memo treats as a risk to investigate. Sourced for central-district and pre-war-build coverage. Verdict band allows Buy because the memo leans that way (recorded Borderline). Yield band 5.1–6.8 brackets the comp p25–p75 (≈5.3–6.3%) the agent picks its rent benchmark from. Risk substrings: elevated czynsz (20.2 PLN/m²) and the below-comp discount.

Outer tier with the suite's heaviest supply pipeline: a 53 m² 3-room 2025 new-build, top floor (6/6), on ul. Instalatorów in Raków, Włochy. (Sourced under a working 'Ochota' label that was wrong — the listing is Włochy/Raków; the case id and notes are corrected here.) Asking 1,060,000 PLN (19,996 PLN/m²), ~21% above the comp median. Sourced for new-build and extreme-supply coverage (Włochy 52.3 completions/1,000 residents). Yield band 4.2–5.7 brackets the comp p25–p75; the agent chose the p75 rent benchmark, so the recorded 5.38% sits near the top. Risk substrings: price premium and supply pipeline.

Transit-strong outer-inner tier: a 62 m² 3-room flat in a 2008 block on ul. Górczewska, Bemowo, ~400 m from the M2 metro. Asking 979,000 PLN (15,911 PLN/m²) — fair vs comps, but czynsz of 1,400 PLN (22.7 PLN/m²) is the heaviest in the suite and drags net yield to ~3.3%. Sourced as a metro-proximity contrast to the outer cases. Yield band 4.3–5.6 brackets the comp p25–p75 (≈4.5–5.4%). Risk substrings: extreme czynsz and metro (the memo also flags the listing's overstated '1 minute to metro' claim).

Outer tier, private seller: a 46 m² 2-room flat in a 2018 MintCity block on ul. Budowlana, Bródno, Targówek. Asking 860,000 PLN (18,655 PLN/m²), ~31% above the comp median — the steepest premium in the suite. Carries an active mortgage to be discharged at sale. Sourced for outer-district and private-seller coverage. Yield band 3.8–5.0 brackets the comp p25–p75; the agent chose the p75 rent benchmark, so the recorded 4.70% sits at the top. Risk substrings: price premium and the mortgage encumbrance.
